webhooks.name: mraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container for Ray Dashboard authentication and mTLS init containers, volumes, and environment variables for encrypted inter-node communication; validates on create and update that these injected components (OAuth proxy, TLS volumes, service account, cert init containers, env vars) have not been tampered with and that native ingress is not enabled.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container for Ray Dashboard authentication and mTLS init containers, volumes, and environment variables for encrypted inter-node communication; validates on create and update that these injected components (OAuth proxy, TLS volumes, service account, cert init containers, env vars) have not been tampered with and that native ingress is not enabled.

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent within a runtime and unique across runtimes sharing the same protocol version, and to enforce multi-node WorkerSpec constraints such as valid GPU types, parallel size minimums, disallowed env overrides, single-container limits, and preventing removal of an existing WorkerSpec.

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods owned by KServe InferenceServices at creation time, injecting storage initializer containers (for model downloading), credential volumes/env vars (for accessing model storage), inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, and modelcar (OCI image source) init containers as needed.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for LLMInferenceService (v1alpha1) that enforces structural invariants on create and update: mutual exclusivity of HTTPRoute refs vs inline spec and their compatibility with gateway configuration, parallelism constraints (worker requires parallelism, pipeline and data parallelism are mutually exclusive, data/dataLocal must be set together, positive values), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, exactly one of inline or ref), scaling rules (delegated to v1alpha2), and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService resources on create and update, enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism settings (pipeline vs data parallelism mutual exclusivity, data/dataLocal pairing), immutability of total parallelism size across updates, scheduler config completeness, and autoscaling rules including replica bounds, WVA actuator backend exclusivity (HPA vs KEDA), actuator consistency between decode and prefill workloads, variantCost format, and KEDA controller-owned field restrictions.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by rejecting specs that use the forbidden baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled) and ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero. It also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by forbidding the baseRefs field (disabling recursive refs), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, and running any injected validation function. It also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent within a runtime and unique across runtimes sharing the same protocol version, and to enforce multi-node WorkerSpec constraints such as valid GPU types, parallel size minimums, disallowed env overrides, single-container limits, and preventing removal of an existing WorkerSpec.

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates Pods created for KServe InferenceServices by injecting storage initializer containers (for model downloading), credential volumes/env vars for accessing model storage, inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, and Istio CNI security contexts.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 resources on create and update, enforcing router/HTTPRoute cross-field constraints (e.g., refs vs inline spec mutual exclusivity, managed vs custom gateway compatibility), parallelism constraints (data/pipeline parallelism mutual exclusivity, required co-presence of data and dataLocal, positive values), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, exactly one of inline or ref), scaling rules via delegation to v1alpha2, and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService resources on create and update, enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism settings (pipeline vs data parallelism, immutability of total parallelism size), scheduler config (inline vs ref exclusivity, replica bounds), and autoscaling rules (HPA/KEDA mutual exclusivity, actuator consistency between decode and prefill workloads, WVA variant cost format, KEDA idle replica semantics).

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, ensuring baseRefs is not set (recursive refs are disabled) and scheduler replicas are greater than zero. It also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by rejecting specs that include baseRefs (recursive refs are disabled), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, running any injected validation function, and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime create/update requests by ensuring model format priorities are consistent within a runtime and unique across runtimes in the same namespace that share protocol versions, and by validating multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (GPU types, parallel sizes, container counts, blocked env vars).

webhooks.name: notebooks-validation.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates Notebook update requests to prevent removal of the MLflow instance annotation while a notebook is running, because removing it deletes the RoleBinding immediately while environment variables persist until pod restart, creating a broken state where the MLflow client has credentials but no permissions.

webhooks.name: notebooks.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs that enriches notebook pods on create/update: it injects a reconciliation-lock annotation, resolves container images from OpenShift ImageStreams, mounts CA certificate bundles and pipeline-runtime-images ConfigMaps, syncs and mounts Elyra pipeline configuration secrets from DSPA, injects a kube-rbac-proxy authentication sidecar, injects cluster-wide proxy and MLflow tracking environment variables, and blocks pod-template-changing mutations on running (non-stopped) notebooks to prevent unintended pod restarts.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific network security defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it sets the secure trusted network annotation to true and forcibly disables Ingress/Route creation (overriding user settings) to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the annotation to false.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific network security defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it sets the secure trusted network annotation to true and forcibly disables Ingress/Route creation (overriding user settings) to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the annotation to false.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates RayCluster resources on create and update, ensuring the cluster name matches a strict DNS-compatible regex pattern (lowercase alphanumeric, starting with a letter) and that all worker group names within the spec are unique.

webhooks.name: vcohort.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates Cohort resources on create and update by checking the spec's fairSharing and resourceGroups fields against validation rules, ensuring cohort configurations are well-formed before being persisted. No mutations are performed (defaulting is a no-op).

webhooks.name: mmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for ModelRegistry custom resources. The mutating handler applies default values to the CR spec on create/update, while the validating handler enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness on creation (by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources) and validates the registry spec fields on both create and update.

webhooks.name: vmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for ModelRegistry custom resources. The mutating handler applies default values to the CR spec on create/update, while the validating handler enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness on creation (by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources) and validates the registry spec fields on both create and update.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.modelmesh-webhook-server.default
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources that enforces autoscaler configuration rules on multi-model (ModelMesh-compatible) runtimes. It validates that the autoscaler class is a supported type (e.g. HPA), HPA metrics are from the allowed list, target utilization percentage is an integer between 1-100, and that explicit replicas are not set simultaneously with an enabled autoscaler, along with min/max replica consistency.

webhooks.name: minferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates InferenceGraph resources on create/update to ensure that serviceURL references in graph node steps use only cluster-internal hostnames and target services within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security. The mutating defaulter is a no-op placeholder.

webhooks.name: minferenceservice-v1beta1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates that InferenceService resources are not created in the protected applications namespace (determined by the DSCInitialization CR) and enforces name-length constraints, while providing a no-op mutating defaulter. This prevents users from deploying inference services into the operator's own namespace.

webhooks.name: mutating.pod.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates newly created Pods that have RAY_USE_TLS enabled on their kserve-container or worker-container by injecting TLS-related volumes, volume mounts, and an init container that copies Ray TLS certificates from a shared secret into the pod, enabling encrypted communication between Ray cluster nodes in KServe inference deployments.

webhooks.name: validating.isvc.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates that InferenceService resources are not created in the protected applications namespace (determined by the DSCInitialization CR) and enforces name-length constraints, while providing a no-op mutating defaulter. This prevents users from deploying inference services into the operator's own namespace.

webhooks.name: validating.nim.account.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates NIM Account creation by enforcing a singleton constraint: only one Account resource is allowed per namespace. Updates and deletions are always permitted without additional validation.

webhooks.name: vinferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates InferenceGraph resources on create/update to ensure that serviceURL references in graph node steps use only cluster-internal hostnames and target services within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, preventing cross-namespace references and public hostname usage for security. The mutating defaulter is a no-op placeholder.

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces, looks up the owning SparkApplication CR by the pod's spark-app-name label, and patches the pod spec with Spark-specific configuration (volumes, volume mounts, env vars, config maps, owner references, memory limits, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, DNS config, scheduler name, priority class, security contexts, sidecar/init containers, GPU resources, host networking, termination grace period, lifecycle hooks, host aliases, container ports, and Prometheus monitoring config) derived from the SparkApplication spec, differentiating between driver and executor pods.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources when they are created or updated. It only applies defaults when the application is in a 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, delegating to the operator's scheme-level defaulting logic to populate unset fields with sensible defaults.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update, ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label so that downstream resources like Services and child SparkApplications can be created without naming conflicts. The validate method for deeper business-logic checks is stubbed out (TODO).

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates SparkApplication resources on create and update by enforcing that the name is DNS-1035 compliant, the spec has no conflicting node selectors or duplicate driver ingress options, pod templates require Spark >= 3.0.0, and optionally checks that requested resources fit within the namespace's ResourceQuotas.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v2 resources on create and delete. On create, enforces a singleton constraint by denying the request if a DSCInitialization instance already exists. On delete, prevents removal while any DataScienceCluster resource still exists, ensuring the initialization resource outlives all dependent clusters.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs that validates and injects data-connection secrets. It parses the 'opendatahub.io/connections' annotation for secret references, verifies each secret exists in the notebook's namespace, confirms the requesting user has RBAC 'get' permission on those secrets via SubjectAccessReview, then patches the notebook container's envFrom list to add or remove the corresponding secretRef entries.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates create and delete operations on DSCInitialization v1 resources. On create, it enforces a singleton constraint by denying the request if a DSCInitialization instance already exists. On delete, it prevents removal of the DSCInitialization while any DataScienceCluster object still exists in the cluster.

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts create/update of Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware profile settings (resource requests/limits, nodeSelector, tolerations) from a referenced HardwareProfile CR into the workload's pod spec. It also handles profile switching (clearing old settings before applying new ones) and profile removal (cleaning up injected settings when the annotation is removed).

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects model-serving connection details (storage credentials, URIs, service accounts) into the ISVC spec based on a platform connection annotation, supporting S3, OCI, and URI connection types with full lifecycle management (inject on create, update/replace/remove on update).

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for LLMInferenceService resources that automatically injects model connection details (model URI, image pull secrets, service account) based on an opendatahub.io/connections annotation referencing a Secret. It supports URI/HuggingFace, S3, and OCI connection types, handling injection on create, and cleanup/replacement on update when the connection annotation changes.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that automatically injects the opendatahub.io/monitoring=true label onto PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources created or updated in namespaces labeled for ODH monitoring, after verifying the cluster-scoped Monitoring CR exists (i.e., monitoring is enabled).

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults fields on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create and update. When ModelRegistry is Managed and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it sets RegistriesNamespace to the default value.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 resources on create and update. Enforces singleton semantics by denying creation if another DataScienceCluster instance already exists, and rejects any DSC where the Kueue component managementState is set to Managed (no longer supported).

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v1 resources during create and update. Specifically, when ModelRegistry is in Managed state and RegistriesNamespace is empty, it defaults RegistriesNamespace to the operator's built-in default namespace.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 admission requests: on create, enforces singleton (only one DSC instance allowed) and denies Kueue with Managed state; on update, denies Kueue Managed state and prevents v1 API modifications when v2-only components are currently Managed (to avoid silent data loss during v1-to-v2 conversion).

webhooks.name: vworkspace.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for Workspace resources on create and update. It fetches the referenced WorkspaceKind to verify it exists, then validates that the selected imageConfig and podConfig IDs are defined in that WorkspaceKind's allowed values, and that pod metadata labels/annotations are well-formed.

webhooks.name: vworkspacekind.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for WorkspaceKind resources that enforces structural correctness on create (pod metadata, env vars, imageConfig/podConfig values, redirect cycles, port references, defaults) and on update additionally prevents modification or removal of imageConfig/podConfig options that are actively in use by Workspace instances, while on delete it blocks deletion if any Workspaces still reference the WorkspaceKind or if a protection finalizer is present.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist as LocalModelNodeGroup resources. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService in the namespace still references the cache via its labels.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates Ray RayCluster resources for Kueue integration: the mutating webhook applies default local queue names, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue, and sets the managed-by field; the validating webhook rejects clusters that enable in-tree autoscaling, have more than 7 worker groups, use the reserved 'head' group name, or violate topology-aware scheduling or queue-name immutability constraints.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates Ray RayCluster resources for Kueue integration: the mutating webhook applies default local queue names, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue, and sets the managed-by field; the validating webhook rejects clusters that enable in-tree autoscaling, have more than 7 worker groups, use the reserved 'head' group name, or violate topology-aware scheduling or queue-name immutability constraints.

webhooks.name: mmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue label, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace selectors), and sets the managedBy field for MultiKueue; the validating webhook enforces queue-name consistency on create/update and validates topology-aware scheduling annotations on each replica spec.

webhooks.name: vmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue label, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace selectors), and sets the managedBy field for MultiKueue; the validating webhook enforces queue-name consistency on create/update and validates topology-aware scheduling annotations on each replica spec.

webhooks.name: mjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for JobSet resources in the Kueue scheduling system. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership chains and namespace selectors), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook ensures queue-name label consistency, validates topology-aware scheduling annotations on replicated job pod templates, and prevents immutable field changes on update.

webhooks.name: vjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for JobSet resources in the Kueue scheduling system. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership chains and namespace selectors), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook ensures queue-name label consistency, validates topology-aware scheduling annotations on replicated job pod templates, and prevents immutable field changes on update.

webhooks.name: mstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for StatefulSets integrated with Kueue job queueing. The mutating webhook propagates queue-name labels/annotations onto pod templates and suspends StatefulSets that should be managed by Kueue, while the validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability when pods are running, prevents pod spec changes while suspended, and restricts replica scaling to only scale-to-zero or scale-from-zero.

webhooks.name: vstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for StatefulSets integrated with Kueue job queueing. The mutating webhook propagates queue-name labels/annotations onto pod templates and suspends StatefulSets that should be managed by Kueue, while the validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability when pods are running, prevents pod spec changes while suspended, and restricts replica scaling to only scale-to-zero or scale-from-zero.

webhooks.name: mleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission control for LeaderWorkerSet resources integrated with Kueue. The mutating webhook applies default local queue labels, suspends workloads that Kueue should manage, and annotates leader/worker pod templates with suspension-by-parent and group-serving metadata. The validating webhook enforces queue name validity, topology-aware scheduling annotation correctness, and immutability of the queue name, priority class, and pod template specs on updates.

webhooks.name: vleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission control for LeaderWorkerSet resources integrated with Kueue. The mutating webhook applies default local queue labels, suspends workloads that Kueue should manage, and annotates leader/worker pod templates with suspension-by-parent and group-serving metadata. The validating webhook enforces queue name validity, topology-aware scheduling annotation correctness, and immutability of the queue name, priority class, and pod template specs on updates.

webhooks.name: mrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission control for KubeRay RayJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs so Kueue controls admission, and sets the managedBy field; the validating webhook enforces that Kueue-managed RayJobs shut down their cluster after finishing, do not reference existing clusters or use autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups with no group named "head", and carry valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: vrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission control for KubeRay RayJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook defaults the local queue name, suspends jobs so Kueue controls admission, and sets the managedBy field; the validating webhook enforces that Kueue-managed RayJobs shut down their cluster after finishing, do not reference existing clusters or use autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups with no group named "head", and carry valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: mresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ResourceFlavor resources. The mutating webhook ensures a 'ResourceInUse' finalizer is added on creation, while the validating webhook checks that nodeLabels, nodeTaints, and tolerations conform to Kubernetes labeling and taint rules (valid label names/values, unique taints by key+effect pair).

webhooks.name: vresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ResourceFlavor resources. The mutating webhook ensures a 'ResourceInUse' finalizer is added on creation, while the validating webhook checks that nodeLabels, nodeTaints, and tolerations conform to Kubernetes labeling and taint rules (valid label names/values, unique taints by key+effect pair).

webhooks.name: mclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer to new ClusterQueues, and validating webhook enforces structural correctness on create/update — checking resource group uniqueness, flavor quota validity, borrowing/lending limit constraints (e.g., lending ≤ nominal, limits nil when no cohort), admission check exclusivity, preemption policy consistency, namespace selector syntax, and fair sharing settings.

webhooks.name: vclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer to new ClusterQueues, and validating webhook enforces structural correctness on create/update — checking resource group uniqueness, flavor quota validity, borrowing/lending limit constraints (e.g., lending ≤ nominal, limits nil when no cohort), admission check exclusivity, preemption policy consistency, namespace selector syntax, and fair sharing settings.

webhooks.name: mworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue Workload resources. The mutating webhook strips minCount from pod sets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on pod sets (e.g., reserved resource keys, at most one variable-count pod set), admission status immutability after quota reservation, reclaimable pod counts, and admission check pod-set updates.

webhooks.name: vworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue Workload resources. The mutating webhook strips minCount from pod sets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on pod sets (e.g., reserved resource keys, at most one variable-count pod set), admission status immutability after quota reservation, reclaimable pod counts, and admission check pod-set updates.

webhooks.name: validator.pytorchjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PyTorchJob resources on create and update, enforcing naming conventions (DNS-1035), replica spec constraints (Master must have exactly 1 replica, Worker at least 1), required 'pytorch' default container with a non-empty image, elastic policy consistency (NProcPerNode deprecation and mutual exclusion), and RunPolicy validity.

webhooks.name: validator.xgboostjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates XGBoostJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that replica specs contain only valid types (Master/Worker), that exactly one Master replica exists, and that every replica spec includes at least one container named 'xgboost' with a defined image.

webhooks.name: validator.tfjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TFJob resources on create and update, enforcing that the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that TFReplicaSpecs are present with at least one container named 'tensorflow' per replica that has an image defined, and that at most one Chief or Master replica type exists.

webhooks.name: validator.jaxjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates JAXJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that jaxReplicaSpecs are present and only use the 'Worker' replica type, that each replica spec has containers with images defined, and that at least one container is named 'jax'.

webhooks.name: validator.paddlejob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PaddleJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, replica types are limited to Master and Worker, each replica spec has containers with images defined, and at least one container is named 'paddle'. On updates, it also validates RunPolicy immutability constraints.

webhooks.name: vogxserver.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for OGXServer custom resources on create and update. It enforces that the distribution name is known, provider IDs are unique, model-to-provider references resolve to declared providers, and adoption annotations do not equal the CR name (which would cause Deployment name conflicts).

webhooks.name: validator.clustertrainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates ClusterTrainingRuntime resources on create and update by checking that replicated jobs in the runtime spec are valid, and warns if the runtime is marked as deprecated via labels.

webhooks.name: validator.trainjob.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainJob resources on create and update by resolving the runtime referenced in the TrainJob spec and delegating validation to that runtime's ValidateObjects method, rejecting requests that reference unsupported runtimes.

webhooks.name: validator.trainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainingRuntime resources on creation, ensuring that replicated jobs marked as ancestors (trainer, model-initializer, dataset-initializer) have exactly 1 replica and contain the required container name for their ancestor type (e.g., 'node' for trainer, 'model-initializer' for model-initializer).

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist as LocalModelNodeGroup resources. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService in the namespace still references the cache via its labels.

webhooks.name: conversion.dscinitializations.dscinitialization.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Implements CRD conversion webhook for DSCInitialization, converting between the v1 (spoke) and v2 (hub) API versions. It maps spec fields (ApplicationsNamespace, Monitoring, TrustedCABundle, DevFlags) and status fields bidirectionally so that clients using either API version see a consistent representation of the cluster-scoped DSCInitialization singleton.
